message:
feat(wish): enhance SSH handler and style improvements

Improve wish SSH framework handler functionality and lipgloss style
module. Includes docs E2E test updates and beads sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -27,7 +27,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1i7e","title":"demo_showcase: CLI contract (modes, flags, help)","description":"Define the binary UX for launching the demo.

Proposed modes/flags (finalize based on UX concept):
- default: run interactive local TUI
- `--theme <name|auto>`
- `--theme-file <path>` (optional)
- `--seed <u64>` (stable demo data)
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--no-alt-screen` (run without alt-screen; useful for debugging + demonstrating bubbletea println/printf)
- `--self-check` (headless CI-friendly render + exit)

Optional subcommands:
- `ssh --addr :2222 --host-key <path>` (wish integration; feature-gated)
- `export --html <path>` / `export --plain <path>` (optional: export snapshots)

Acceptance:
- `--help` is clean and self-explanatory.
- Flags map directly to `demo_showcase::Config` fields.
- Error messages are user-friendly (bad theme name, bad seed, missing file).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.810927082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539935175Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539870655Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:05.884573066Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.830556320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1isw","title":"huh: Fix FilePicker single-file selection edge case","description":"# Bug: FilePicker Cannot Select Single File

## Problem Statement
In the FilePicker navigation logic, the down-arrow condition prevents
selecting the only file when there's exactly one file:

```rust
// Lines 3660-3663
if self.selected_index < self.files.len().saturating_sub(1) {
    self.selected_index += 1;
    // ...
}
```

When files.len() == 1:
- saturating_sub(1) = 0
- condition: 0 < 0 = FALSE
- navigation does nothing

## Root Cause Analysis
The condition `< len - 1` is meant to prevent going past the last item,
but it also prevents selecting the first (and only) item when starting
from index 0.

Actually, the bug is in the logic design. The condition should allow
moving TO the last item, not just items BEFORE the last.

Wait, re-reading: if selected_index is 0 and len is 1:
- Can't move down because 0 < 0 is false
But the item at index 0 IS selectable - it's already selected by default.

The real issue might be different - let me trace the full logic.

## Actual Issue
The condition prevents moving DOWN when already at last item, which is correct.
But if the initial state has selected_index pointing elsewhere or if there's
a reset that doesn't account for single-item lists, selection could be broken.

Let me re-examine:
```rust
// When len=1, saturating_sub(1)=0
// selected_index starts at 0 (presumably)
// Down key: 0 < 0 is false, no change (correct - already at end)
// Up key: 0 > 0 is false, no change (correct - already at start)
```

This is actually correct for navigation. The bug might be elsewhere -
perhaps in initialization or the actual selection/confirm logic.

## Further Investigation Needed
1. Check initial selected_index value for single-file directories
2. Check if Enter/confirm works with single file
3. Check if the file is visually highlighted

## Potential Fix Areas
If init doesn't set selected_index correctly:
```rust
fn set_files(&mut self, files: Vec<PathBuf>) {
    self.files = files;
    self.selected_index = 0.min(self.files.len().saturating_sub(1));
}
```

If confirm doesn't work:
```rust
fn confirm(&self) -> Option<PathBuf> {
    self.files.get(self.selected_index).cloned()
}
```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn filepicker_single_file() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/single-file-dir");
    // Simulate single file
    picker.files = vec![PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")];
    picker.selected_index = 0;
    
    // Should be able to select it
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("only_file.txt"));
    assert!(/* file is highlighted */);
    
    // Confirm should return the file
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert_eq!(selected, Some(PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")));
}

#[test]
fn filepicker_empty_directory() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/empty-dir");
    picker.files = vec![];
    
    // Should handle gracefully
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("No files") || view.is_empty() == false);
    
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert!(selected.is_none());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single-file directories work correctly
- [ ] Empty directories handled gracefully
- [ ] Navigation works for 0, 1, and many files
- [ ] Selection/confirm works in all cases
- [ ] Tests added for edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.943176512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:25:23.732034528Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:25:23.731965359Z","close_reason":"Fixed FilePicker edge cases: empty files guard on down-nav and goto_bottom, height underflow protection with saturating_sub, added 15 comprehensive unit tests covering single-file, empty-directory, height=0/1, navigation bounds, selectability defaults, and format_size.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edge-case","filepicker","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1isw","depends_on_id":"bd-2xzk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.967192347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1knu","title":"Quality: headless self-check mode (CI-friendly)","description":"Add a non-interactive mode (e.g., `demo_showcase --self-check`) that validates the demo can boot and render headlessly.

Why:
- CI needs a fast "does it basically work" signal without a real TTY.
- Self-check also prevents subtle regressions in bootstrap/theming/rendering.

What self-check does:
- Construct the Config (deterministic defaults).
- Bootstrap the App model.
- Render a small set of views/pages (at a fixed size), for example:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
- Optionally run a tiny scripted navigation (no user input required).

Output:
- On success: exit 0.
- On failure:
  - exit non-zero
  - print a concise error + where artifacts were written
  - write a small artifact bundle (last rendered frame, config snapshot) under `target/demo_showcase_e2e/` or similar.

Constraints:
- Must work in `TERM=dumb` / no-tty environments.
- Must never hang (max-step/time guard).

Acceptance:
- Self-check runs quickly and deterministically in CI.
- Failures are actionable without rerunning locally.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:29.630984785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T16:54:34.964049662Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T16:54:34.963982256Z","close_reason":"Implemented headless self-check mode using E2ERunner. The --self-check flag navigates through Dashboard, Jobs, Logs, and Docs pages, validates each renders correctly, and exits 0 on success. Works in CI environments without a TTY.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-13np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:35.814496203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:26.513764136Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:29.649380944Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1knu","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:29.218612410Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1kx7","title":"E2E: extend CI script to run full scenario suite","description":"Extend the E2E runner script to run the full demo_showcase scenario suite (not just smoke), with great logging and CI-friendly controls.

Why:
- The demo_showcase is a huge integration surface; regressions will happen.
- A single, well-instrumented E2E entrypoint makes it easy to validate “everything still works” locally and in CI.

Requirements:
- Profiles:
  - `smoke` (fast): minimal navigation, no panics, basic rendering assertions.
  - `full` (slower): runs every scenario and validates key outputs/artifacts.
  - optional `nightly` (extra slow): stress/resizes, multiple seeds, async-enabled run.
- Scenario selection:
  - allow selecting a subset (e.g., `--scenario docs` or `--scenario wizard,logs`).
- Determinism:
  - accepts a seed (or chooses and prints one) and writes it into the artifact directory.
- Observability:
  - structured JSONL log output + per-step timing
  - on failure, print a clear summary including artifact path and last N steps
- Exit codes:
  - non-zero on failure; zero on success.

Acceptance:
- CI can choose which profile to run (PR vs nightly) without editing the script.
- Failures are actionable: the artifact directory contains enough info to reproduce.

Testing:
- The script itself is validated by running at least the smoke suite in CI.
- Full suite runs either on demand or on nightly schedule.

Notes:
- Keep this script the canonical entrypoint; avoid multiple half-overlapping scripts.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.825203081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:28.537686113Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-1g80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:31.237245937Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3449","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:42.979497295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ecr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:29.940423979Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3nrc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:41.602626554Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3spt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:32.598620003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-6wkl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:28.537657400Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-miqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:44.326429546Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.846212242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Notes:
- Keep this script the canonical entrypoint; avoid multiple half-overlapping scripts.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.825203081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:28:12.064646559Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","script","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-1g80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:31.237245937Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3449","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:42.979497295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ecr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:29.940423979Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3nrc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:41.602626554Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-3spt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:01:32.598620003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-6wkl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:51:28.537657400Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-miqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:44.326429546Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kx7","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:27.846212242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m7x","title":"E2E scenario: ssh mode smoke (optional/manual)","description":"Optional E2E scenario for wish integration.

Constraints:
- This may be too heavy for default CI.

Plan:
- Generate/use a temp host key.
- Start `demo_showcase ssh` on localhost with an ephemeral port.
- Connect with an SSH client (or a minimal russh client) and ensure:
  - the server accepts a session
  - the UI renders at least one frame
  - a quit sequence cleanly closes the session

Logging:
- Capture server logs + client transcript to artifacts.

Acceptance:
- Provides high confidence that wish integration stays alive over time.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:31.040331959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:33.585303389Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","ssh","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-32ba","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:33.585268063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-3xs0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:32.328836611Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m7x","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:31.067983923Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1nwu","title":"Settings: toggles (mouse, animations, no-color, syntax)","description":"Expose runtime toggles:
- mouse on/off
- animations on/off
- force ASCII/no color
- syntax highlighting + line numbers (if enabled)

Implementation notes:
- This page should mutate the live app state, but also reflect the initial defaults from `demo_showcase::Config`.

Acceptance:
- Toggling updates app behavior live without restart.
- Toggles are discoverable and have immediate, visible effects (not hidden flags).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:01.250474646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T16:56:54.222802995Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T16:56:54.222719740Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:01.272885316Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:54.907924170Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:38.554374326Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2szb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:00.214777601Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:02.860339836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1o6y","title":"Implement huh Multiselect, Text, FilePicker, Note field types","description":"Implement missing Huh field types: Multiselect, FilePicker, and Note. (Textarea is tracked separately under bd-212m.4.1.)

MultiSelect and Note were already present. All field types have builder pattern, validation, keyboard handling, theme support, and view rendering.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-39zu","title":"demo_showcase: unified theming + styling system (lipgloss)","description":"Make the demo visually cohesive and "turned up to 11" by building a real theming system on top of lipgloss.

Deliverables:
- ThemeContext-driven styles (semantic slots: primary/success/warn/error/muted/border/etc).
- Live theme switching (multiple presets + maybe custom) without UI glitches.
- Shared layout helpers: app chrome, cards, tables, badges, buttons, toasts.

Acceptance:
- Every page uses the same style primitives; switching theme updates the whole app.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:05.727815830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:05.746609025Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39zu","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:05.746566305Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3aio","title":"Jobs: timer/stopwatch for durations + ETA","description":"Add realistic time semantics to Jobs: durations, ETAs, and timers.

Why:
- CI/ops dashboards live and die by good time cues ("how long", "how much left").
- This showcases `bubbles::stopwatch`/`bubbles::timer` in a product context.

What to show:
- Per-job:
  - start time
  - elapsed duration (stopwatch)
  - ETA / remaining time (timer or derived estimate)
- Global:
  - an overall "pipeline" timer (optional)

Integration:
- Use bubbletea tick/every for updates (no wall-clock sleeps).
- Tie to job state transitions:
  - stop timers when job completes
  - freeze time when job is paused/cancelled

Acceptance:
- Time displays are stable, readable, and update smoothly.
- No flakiness in tests (no dependence on real time).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:23.055080227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:21.017175139Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","timer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3aio","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:23.075813532Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3aio","depends_on_id":"bd-3m25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:05.033421770Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3aio","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:07.636146806Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

The underlying discrepancies had already been fixed - just removed outdated skip_reason entries from fixtures.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-3b7o","title":"Settings: keybindings reference + customization hooks","description":"Display a comprehensive keybindings reference inside Settings (and optionally link to it from the help overlay).

Why:
- The demo must be self-teaching. Users should never feel stuck or need to read source.
- A single canonical keymap also prevents "random per-page keys" that make TUIs feel amateurish.

What to show:
- Global keys (quit, help, back/close, focus next/prev, search/command palette, theme switch, guided tour).
- Page-specific shortcuts (Dashboard drill-down, Jobs start/cancel, Logs follow, Docs search, Files open/preview).
- Interaction conventions:
  - list/table navigation
  - viewport scrolling
  - form submit/cancel

Nice-to-have:
- Show the *effective* keymap (reflecting feature flags/toggles):
  - if command palette not built, omit it
  - if mouse disabled, hide mouse tips

Acceptance:
- The key reference is readable, nicely styled, and matches the actual behavior.
- The app feels self-teaching: new users can explore without guessing.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:05.551180539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:24.199337985Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3b7o","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:10.878758538Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b7o","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:05.585413730Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3b7o","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:08.241305359Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3b7o","title":"Settings: keybindings reference + customization hooks","description":"Display a comprehensive keybindings reference inside Settings (and optionally link to it from the help overlay).

Why:
- The demo must be self-teaching. Users should never feel stuck or need to read source.
- A single canonical keymap also prevents "random per-page keys" that make TUIs feel amateurish.

What to show:
- Global keys (quit, help, back/close, focus next/prev, search/command palette, theme switch, guided tour).
- Page-specific shortcuts (Dashboard drill-down, Jobs start/cancel, Logs follow, Docs search, Files open/preview).
- Interaction conventions:
  - list/table navigation
  - viewport scrolling
  - form submit/cancel

Nice-to-have:
- Show the *effective* keymap (reflecting feature flags/toggles):
  - if command palette not built, omit it
  - if mouse disabled, hide mouse tips

Acceptance:
- The key reference is readable, nicely styled, and matches the actual behavior.

 {"id":"bd-3chy","title":"demo_showcase: optional SSH server mode (wish)","description":"Add an optional mode that serves the showcase over SSH (via `wish`) to prove the remote/SSH story is viable.

Why:
- Wish is one of the Charm ecosystem’s most compelling differentiators.
- Serving the same UI over SSH demonstrates composability: bubbletea Program IO abstraction + wish sessions + terminal capability negotiation.

Requirements:
- CLI:
  - `demo_showcase --ssh <addr>` (e.g., `:2222` or `0.0.0.0:2222`) starts the server.
  - host key UX:
    - accept `--host-key <path>` and/or generate a key if missing (decide in child tasks)
- Session behavior:
  - each SSH connection gets its own app instance (or a curated subset if full UI is too heavy)
  - wire bubbletea input/output to the SSH session streams
  - handle PTY sizing and resize events (best-effort)
- Security/ergonomics:
  - basic auth/middleware polish (simple but not embarrassing)
  - clear logs + startup banner (address, auth mode, host key path)

Acceptance:
- Running the server and connecting with `ssh -p 2222 localhost` launches the UI and is usable.
- Works with minimal setup beyond a host key.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - argument parsing and config wiring for ssh mode
  - host-key path validation logic
- E2E scripts:
  - a CI-safe “handshake + banner” test (non-interactive) that proves the server starts and accepts connections.
  - a local “interactive smoke” script (optional) that drives basic navigation via SSH and captures logs/artifacts.

Notes:
- Keep this optional: the main value of demo_showcase should not depend on SSH being enabled.
- Be realistic about CI environments; gate the heavier SSH tests appropriately.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.593755071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:44:44.471178493Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ssh","wish"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3chy","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:12.612205255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3d1w","title":"Dashboard: mouse affordances (clickable cards)","description":"Add mouse affordances to the Dashboard so it feels like a modern interactive console.

Why:
- Mouse support is an "advanced" feature that many terminal apps skip.
- When done well (subtle highlights + click actions), it makes the demo feel shockingly polished.

Behaviors:
- Click to select/focus a dashboard card/widget.
- Click on obvious affordances (e.g., "View logs" / "Open job") to navigate.
- Hover/selection highlight (keyboard focus and mouse selection should be visually consistent).

Constraints:
- Must degrade gracefully when mouse is disabled (Config toggle) or terminal doesn’t support it.
- Wheel scroll should not break layout; only scroll the active viewport/list when applicable.

Acceptance:
- Click selects a card and visibly focuses it.
- Mouse interactions never steal focus unexpectedly or cause layout corruption.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:01.759013697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:38:54.253286979Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:38:54.253217098Z","close_reason":"Implemented dashboard mouse affordances: clickable cards with visual selection highlighting, keyboard navigation (j/k/Tab/arrows), Enter to navigate to Services/Jobs pages, mouse click detection with hit testing, and 4 new tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-3ps4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:25.679670153Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:01.778296349Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d1w","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:24.421135419Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-o39u","title":"Integration Tests: bubbletea + bubbles","description":"Integration tests for bubbles components within bubbletea event loop.

## Purpose
Verify that bubble components work correctly in the full TUI context,
not just in isolation.

## Test Requirements

### Component in Model
- [ ] TextInput receives and handles key events
- [ ] Viewport scrolls with mouse wheel
- [ ] Spinner animates with ticks
- [ ] Progress updates from async commands

### Focus Management
- [ ] Tab navigation between components
- [ ] Focus styling changes correctly
- [ ] Blur handlers fire appropriately

### Command Integration
- [ ] Component commands execute via batch
- [ ] Timer ticks reach components
- [ ] Mouse events route to correct component

### Rendering Pipeline
- [ ] Component view() in parent view()
- [ ] Style composition doesn't corrupt
- [ ] Nested component rendering

## Test Approach
Use bubbletea simulator with multi-component Models.
Verify full update/view cycle for realistic scenarios.

## Success Criteria
- Components work identically in isolation and integration
- No event routing bugs
- No rendering artifacts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:24.855009868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:24.205506665Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","bubbletea","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o39u","depends_on_id":"bd-1u1s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:21.687839434Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-o39u","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:24.877440549Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-o39u","depends_on_id":"bd-9sjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:24.205474766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pfqw","title":"demo_showcase: e2e test harness + scripts","description":"Build comprehensive end-to-end verification for the demo_showcase binary.

Why:
- The showcase is the "integration demo" for the whole workspace; it must be reliably testable.
- We want confidence that *everything still works* after refactors.

Constraints:
- E2E must run in CI without a real TTY.
- Avoid flaky sleeps/timeouts.

Strategy:
- Use bubbletea custom I/O + deterministic tick driving.
- Use a scripted input runner to navigate the UI and trigger actions.
- Capture output frames and structured logs as artifacts.

Definition of done:
- `cargo test -p demo_showcase` runs E2E scenarios quickly (<~10s) and deterministically.
- On failure, logs clearly explain which scenario/step failed and why.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.143197913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.237604388Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pfqw","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:28.237574673Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-qkxb","title":"Dashboard: drill-down details modal/panel","description":"Add an interaction to open a drill-down details view for a selected service/deployment/job directly from the Dashboard.

- Side panel (right) that can be toggled open/closed

  - `esc` closes

 {"id":"bd-wbyt","title":"bubbletea: Track async batch commands with TaskTracker for clean shutdown","description":"# Task: Track Async Batch Commands with TaskTracker

## Problem Statement
In the async event loop, batch commands use bare tokio::spawn() instead of
the TaskTracker:

```rust
// Lines 1293-1303
if let Some(batch) = msg.downcast::<BatchMsg>() {
    for cmd in batch.0 {
        let tx_clone = tx.clone();
        // Note: batch commands don't get tracked individually
        tokio::spawn(async move {  // NOT tracked!
            let cmd_kind: CommandKind = cmd.into();
            if let Some(msg) = cmd_kind.execute().await {
                let _ = tx_clone.send(msg).await;
            }
        });
    }
}
```

The comment acknowledges the limitation: "batch commands don't get tracked individually".

## Impact Analysis
- **Delayed shutdown**: Long-running batch commands continue after quit
- **Resource leaks**: Connections/handles held by batch commands not released
- **Inconsistent behavior**: Regular commands tracked, batch commands not
- **Cancellation ignored**: CancellationToken not passed to batch commands

## Current Shutdown Flow
```
1. User quits -> CancellationToken cancelled
2. TaskTracker waits for tracked tasks
3. 5-second timeout, then force exit
4. Untracked batch commands continue running until process dies
```

## Solution Design

### Track All Batch Commands
```rust
if let Some(batch) = msg.downcast::<BatchMsg>() {
    for cmd in batch.0 {
        let tx_clone = tx.clone();
        let cancel = cancel_token.clone();
        
        tracker.spawn(async move {
            tokio::select! {
                result = async {
                    let cmd_kind: CommandKind = cmd.into();
                    cmd_kind.execute().await
                } => {
                    if let Some(msg) = result {
                        let _ = tx_clone.send(msg).await;
                    }
                }
                _ = cancel.cancelled() => {
                    // Command cancelled during shutdown
                    tracing::debug!("Batch command cancelled during shutdown");
                }
            }
        });
    }
}
```

### Benefits
1. All tasks tracked for clean shutdown
2. Cancellation propagated to batch commands
3. Consistent behavior with regular commands
4. Better resource cleanup

## Implementation Steps
1. Pass TaskTracker to batch command handling
2. Pass CancellationToken to batch commands
3. Wrap command execution in select! for cancellation
4. Update tests to verify shutdown behavior
5. Add integration test for batch command cancellation

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn batch_commands_cancelled_on_shutdown() {
    let completed = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));
    let completed_clone = completed.clone();
    
    // Create batch with slow command
    let slow_cmd = AsyncCmd::new(async move {
        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(10)).await;
        completed_clone.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
        None
    });
    
    let program = Program::new(TestModel)
        .with_async();
    
    // Start program and immediately quit
    let handle = tokio::spawn(async move {
        program.run_async().await
    });
    
    // Send batch command then quit
    program.send(Command::batch(vec![slow_cmd]));
    program.quit();
    
    // Should complete quickly, not wait 10 seconds
    let result = tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(2), handle).await;
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    
    // Command should NOT have completed
    assert!(!completed.load(Ordering::SeqCst));
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Batch commands tracked via TaskTracker
- [ ] Cancellation signal propagated to batch commands
- [ ] Clean shutdown doesn't wait for batch commands indefinitely
- [ ] No regression in batch command execution
- [ ] Tests verify cancellation behavior","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:38.899976369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:30:52.223725910Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:30:52.223656361Z","close_reason":"Replaced bare tokio::spawn with tracker.spawn for async batch commands in handle_command_tracked. Batch sub-commands now receive CancellationToken via tokio::select!, ensuring clean shutdown. TaskTracker and cancel_token are cloned before the outer spawn to be available to batch sub-commands. Sequence commands were already inline (no change needed). All 164 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","bubbletea","shutdown","task-tracking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wbyt","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:38.924547759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
